hero.label: Microkernel · Lean 4 · Rust · Formally Verified
hero.subtitle_line1: Every kernel transition is an executable pure function.
hero.subtitle_line2: Every invariant is machine-checked by the Lean type-checker.
hero.h1: seLe4n — A Formally Verified Microkernel Written in Lean 4

about.title: What is seLe4n?
about.lead: seLe4n is a capability-based microkernel whose kernel model is written entirely in Lean 4 with machine-checked proofs, complemented by safe Rust syscall wrappers for user-space programs. It targets the Raspberry Pi 5 (ARM64 / BCM2712) as its first hardware platform.
about.why_microkernel_text: Microkernels minimize the trusted computing base by running only essential services in kernel space — scheduling, IPC, capability management, and memory control. Everything else (drivers, filesystems, networking) runs in user space. This reduces attack surface and makes formal verification tractable: every line of kernel code can be reasoned about mathematically.
about.why_lean_text: Lean 4 is both a general-purpose programming language and an interactive theorem prover. seLe4n leverages this duality: the kernel is executable code whose properties are machine-checked at compile time. There is no separate specification language, no trusted translation step, and no semantic gap between the model and the running code. If it compiles, the proofs hold.
about.how_it_works_title: How It Works: Build → Freeze → Execute
about.how_it_works_p1: seLe4n employs a three-phase architecture. During the Build phase, an IntermediateState wrapping SystemState with invariant witnesses is constructed through pure builder operations (createObject, insertCap, mapPage, registerIrq, registerService). Each builder operation preserves four structural invariants. During the Freeze phase, the one-way freeze function converts the builder state into a FrozenSystemState where all RHTable fields become FrozenMap structures (hash → index + contiguous array) and CNode slots become CNodeRadix flat radix arrays with zero-hash O(1) lookup. During the Execute phase, the frozen state serves all 25 syscalls with value-only mutations — the index map is never modified, all Fin accesses are within bounds by construction, and no fuel or dynamic resizing is needed.
about.how_it_works_p2: All three phases are unified by the KernelM monad, defined as σ → Except ε (α × σ) — a state-transformer with explicit error handling. 18 commutativity theorems prove that frozen_op(freeze(s)) = freeze(builder_op(s)) for value-only mutations, ensuring the frozen execution phase is semantically equivalent to the builder phase. Every transition is deterministic: given the same input state and system call, you always get the same output state.
about.what_different_text: seLe4n unifies implementation and proof in a single language — no separate proof assistant, no translation layer, no semantic gap. Each subsystem follows an Operations/Invariant split: executable transitions live in Operations.lean, and their machine-checked proofs live alongside in Invariant.lean. The type-checker enforces that every invariant holds across every operation.
about.status_text: seLe4n is an active research project at version {{version}}. The kernel model is feature-complete with 25 syscalls spanning IPC, scheduling, capabilities, memory management, notifications, information-flow control, and service orchestration. A companion Rust workspace provides safe, no_std wrappers for all 25 syscalls. Hardware platform bindings for the Raspberry Pi 5 are structurally in place, pending refinement phase closure.
about.created_with: Created thoughtfully with the help of Claude and Codex.

architecture.title: Architecture
architecture.lead: seLe4n follows a layered contract model. Each subsystem's invariant proofs compose upward into a unified apiInvariantBundle that covers the entire kernel. A companion Rust workspace provides safe, no_std syscall wrappers above the API surface.
architecture.diagram_aria: seLe4n architecture diagram showing Rust user-space wrappers, kernel API surface, and layered subsystems down to platform bindings
architecture.prelude_desc: 16 typed identifiers (ObjId, ThreadId, DomainId, Priority, CPtr, Slot, ASID, VAddr, PAddr, CdtNodeId, RegName, etc.) each with Hashable + LawfulHashable instances. The KernelM monad, register files, memory regions, and timer configuration. Sentinel value 0 reserved for ObjId, ThreadId, ServiceId, and CPtr with identity theorems.
architecture.object_model_desc: 7 composable kernel object types: TCB, Endpoint, Notification, CNode, VSpaceRoot, Untyped, SchedContext. 129 theorems across Object (76) and State (53). Objects compose through typed Capability references with bitmask AccessRightSet. The SystemState record aggregates all stores into a single immutable snapshot — HashMap-backed for O(1) access everywhere.
architecture.scheduler_desc: 133 theorems across 6 files. RunQueue backed by HashMap Priority (List ThreadId) with HashSet ThreadId membership index. Priority + EDF scheduling with domain-aware partitioning. schedulerWellFormed structural invariant. Operations split into Core, Preservation, and Selection. O(1) insert, remove, contains, and priority-bucketed candidate selection.
architecture.capability_desc: 118 theorems across 5 modules. CNode slots backed by HashMap Slot Capability for O(1) operations. Node-stable Capability Derivation Tree with childMap : HashMap CdtNodeId (List CdtNodeId). Invariant proofs split into Authority (26 theorems), Defs (46 theorems), and Preservation (46 theorems). Strict revocation via cspaceRevokeCdtStrict.
architecture.ipc_desc: Deeply modularized across 14 files with 223 theorems. Intrusive dual-queue endpoints split into Core, Transport, and WithCaps. 76 structural invariant proofs, 27 endpoint preservation theorems, and 16 capability transfer proofs. Operations decomposed into CapTransfer, Endpoint, and SchedulerLemmas.
architecture.robin_hood_desc: Verified Robin Hood hash table implementation with 179 theorems across 7 modules. RHTable provides O(1) amortized insert, lookup, and erase with open addressing and linear probing. 30 bridge theorems connect the implementation to HashMap-compatible semantics. 56 preservation proofs and 40 lookup correctness theorems. Imported by Model.Object.Types — the verified foundation for all kernel hash-based data structures.
architecture.info_flow_desc: 182 theorems across 9 deeply modularized files. Enforcement split into Soundness and Wrappers. Invariant proofs decomposed into Composition, Helpers, and Operations. 15 non-interference theorems with proven reflexivity and transitivity.
architecture.service_desc: 90 theorems across 4 files. Operations handles registry, interface, and dependency management. Graph-based lifecycle with O(n+e) DFS cycle detection. Invariant proofs split into Acyclicity and Policy enforcement. Dependency-aware startup, shutdown, and restart.
architecture.platform_desc: PlatformBinding typeclass defines runtime, boot, and interrupt boundary contracts. ExtendedBootBoundaryContract adds platform boot fields. Concrete bindings for a simulator and the Raspberry Pi 5 (BCM2712 memory map, GIC-400 constants, ARM64 config, 48-bit VA / 44-bit PA).
architecture.rust_desc: Three no_std Rust crates provide ergonomic, fully safe wrappers around all 25 seLe4n syscalls. sele4n-types defines a 25-variant SyscallId enum, 15 #[repr(transparent)] newtype identifiers, a 44-variant KernelError enum, and bitmask-based AccessRights — all with #![deny(unsafe_code)]. sele4n-abi handles ARM64 register encoding/decoding, IPC buffer overflow, and MessageInfo bitfield packing — with exactly one unsafe block: the inline svc #0 instruction in trap.rs. sele4n-sys exposes typed wrappers for IPC (send/receive/call/reply/replyRecv), CSpace (mint/copy/move/delete), lifecycle (retype), VSpace (map/unmap with W^X enforcement), notification (signal/wait), and service registry (register/revoke/query) — zero unsafe code.

comparison.title: seLe4n vs Traditional Verified Kernels
comparison.lead: seLe4n builds on decades of capability-based microkernel research while introducing architectural improvements enabled by the Lean 4 proof framework.
comparison.language_sele4n: Lean 4 for both implementation and proofs
comparison.verification_gap_sele4n: Zero gap — proofs live alongside code
comparison.user_space_bindings_trad: C headers with manual register marshalling
comparison.data_structures_sele4n: Verified Robin Hood hash map — O(1) hot paths, 179 proofs
comparison.ipc_queuing_sele4n: Dual-queue (sendQ/receiveQ) with O(1) mid-queue removal
comparison.scheduling_sele4n: Priority + EDF with domain-aware partitioning
comparison.cdt_sele4n: Node-stable with HashMap childMap, O(1) slot transfer
comparison.info_flow_sele4n: N-domain two-dimensional labels with per-endpoint policies
comparison.service_mgmt_sele4n: Dependency graph with DFS cycle detection
comparison.revocation_sele4n: Strict variant reporting first failure with context
comparison.syscall_boundary_sele4n: Verified decode with roundtrip + determinism proofs
comparison.user_space_bindings_sele4n: Safe Rust wrappers — 25 syscalls, typed capabilities, zero unsafe in sele4n-sys
comparison.state_model_sele4n: Immutable records, pure functional transitions
comparison.execution_model_sele4n: Three-phase Build → Freeze → Execute with FrozenMap + CNodeRadix
comparison.object_composition_trad: Static object tables, manual pointer management
comparison.object_composition_sele4n: Composable typed capabilities with bitmask rights, 129 structural theorems

features.title: Key Innovations
features.lead: seLe4n introduces architectural improvements across every subsystem, with O(1) data structures and machine-checked correctness at every layer.
features.o1_text: Every hot-path data structure uses hash-based collections backed by a verified Robin Hood hash map with 179 theorems: object stores, run queues, CNode slots, VSpace mappings, IPC queues, ASID tables, CDT child lookups, notification waiters, and service dependency graphs. At freeze time, these become FrozenMap and CNodeRadix structures for deterministic O(1) access. All 16 typed identifiers have Hashable + LawfulHashable instances. 400+ theorems re-verified after the data structure migration.
features.robin_hood_text: Traditional verified kernels rely on linked lists with O(n) traversal. seLe4n builds on a ground-up verified Robin Hood hash table — 179 theorems across 7 modules, with 30 bridge theorems connecting the implementation to HashMap-compatible semantics. This is the verified algebraic foundation for every kernel data structure.
features.scheduler_text: Where traditional kernels use priority-based round-robin, seLe4n combines Priority + EDF with domain-aware partitioning. The RunQueue uses HashMap-backed priority buckets, reducing candidate selection from O(t) total threads to O(k) priority levels. The schedulerWellFormed structural invariant is machine-checked across all 133 theorems.
features.ipc_text: Traditional kernels use intrusive singly-linked lists for IPC queuing. seLe4n endpoints maintain separate sendQ/receiveQ with intrusive links for O(1) mid-queue removal. WithCaps adds verified capability transfer across IPC boundaries — 223 theorems including call-reply-recv composition proofs.
features.cdt_text: Traditional kernels use mutable doubly-linked lists for capability tracking. seLe4n uses a node-stable CDT with HashMap-backed childMap for O(1) slot transfer. Unlike silent error handling in seL4, cspaceRevokeCdtStrict reports the first failure with full context. Bidirectional childMapConsistent invariant ensures the index stays synchronized.
features.info_flow_text: Where traditional kernels use a binary high/low partition, seLe4n implements a parameterized N-domain framework with two-dimensional labels (confidentiality + integrity). DomainFlowPolicy supports arbitrary flow topologies with proven reflexivity and transitivity. 15 non-interference theorems, 7-field ObservableState projection, and precomputed HashSet-based observability for O(1) lookups.
features.service_text: Traditional microkernels have no kernel-level service concept. seLe4n introduces first-class service orchestration with graph-based lifecycle, O(n+e) DFS cycle detection, and dependency-aware startup/shutdown. Invariant proofs split into Acyclicity and Policy enforcement across 90 theorems.
features.arch_text: Where traditional kernels leave register parsing unverified, seLe4n verifies the hardware-software boundary end-to-end. RegisterDecode (18 roundtrip proofs) and SyscallArgDecode (28 determinism proofs) ensure no data is misinterpreted at the trap boundary. TlbModel adds flush-consistency proofs for the memory subsystem.
features.invariant_text: Each subsystem proves its own invariant bundle. Bundles compose upward: coreIpcInvariantBundle, ipcSchedulerCouplingInvariantBundle, lifecycleCompositionInvariantBundle, and vspaceInvariantBundle all roll up into proofLayerInvariantBundle, exposed as the unified apiInvariantBundle. A base-case theorem proves the bundle holds for the default (empty) state.
features.composable_objects_text: Where traditional kernels use static object tables with manual pointer management, seLe4n’s 7 kernel object types compose through typed Capability references with bitmask-based AccessRightSet. All stores in SystemState are HashMap-backed for O(1) access, and the CapDerivationTree enables composable revocation across the entire object graph — 129 theorems total.
features.rust_text: Where traditional kernels expose C headers with manual register marshalling, seLe4n provides three no_std Rust crates with fully safe, typed APIs for all 25 syscalls. sele4n-sys introduces phantom-typed Cap<Obj, Rts> capabilities with compile-time rights tracking and W^X enforcement. The entire crate stack has zero unsafe except the single svc #0 trap instruction in sele4n-abi.

security.title: Security Model
security.lead: Security is woven into every layer of seLe4n through formal information-flow control, capability-based access mediation, and a documented threat model.
security.ifc_text: seLe4n enforces information-flow security at the kernel level through 182 theorems across 9 modules. The DomainFlowPolicy framework provides security guarantees that go beyond traditional binary high/low models:
security.ifc_item2: 7-field ObservableState projection covering full kernel state
security.ifc_item3: Enforcement split into Soundness and Wrappers
security.ifc_item4: Invariant proofs decomposed into Composition, Helpers, Operations
security.ifc_item5: Precomputed observability via HashSet for O(1) projection lookups
security.ifc_item8: Legacy embedding theorem proves the generic system subsumes the original
security.cap_text: All resource access is mediated through capabilities stored in CNode slots. The capability derivation tree tracks all parent-child relationships:
security.cap_item2: Strict revocation with explicit error reporting and context
security.cap_item5: Policy-checked wrappers: endpointSendDualChecked, cspaceMintChecked, registerServiceChecked
security.cap_item6: Sentinel ID rejection at IPC boundaries prevents ghost queue entries
security.threat_item1: External attacker: Public repo access, dependency injection
security.control_item1: Expanded .gitignore secret/artifact denylist
security.control_item2: SHA-256 verified elan installer downloads
security.control_item3: Tier-3 anchor checks against CI workflow semantics

verification.title: Verification & Testing
verification.lead: seLe4n employs a multi-tiered verification strategy combining Lean’s type-checker with runtime testing, ensuring correctness from proof to execution.
verification.tier0_text: Forbidden-marker scans ensure zero sorry and zero axiom across all production code. Documentation link validation, sync checks, and formatting enforcement. The first line of defense.
verification.tier1_text: Full Lean compilation across 266 parallel build jobs. All 2568 theorems are type-checked. Every invariant proof, every bridge lemma, every preservation theorem — including the 18 freeze commutativity proofs — is verified by the Lean type-checker. If it compiles, the proofs hold.
verification.tier2_text: Trace sequence probes validate executable behavior against formal semantics. The negative-state suite tests malformed input rejection across IPC, capabilities, endpoints, and CSpace mutations. Information-flow runtime checks verify enforcement wrappers.
verification.tier3_text: Anchors every named theorem and invariant to the proof surface. Verifies that all documented claims in the Claim & Evidence Index are backed by actual code. Ensures no proof regressions across refactoring. The full correctness gate for PRs.
verification.tier4_text: Determinism checks, experimental feature validation, and long-running invariant probes. Fixture-backed oracle testing. Catches subtle regressions that slip through faster tiers.
verification.low_item2: Trivially true for unchanged state returns

api.title: API Surface
api.lead: The kernel exposes a stable public API through SeLe4n/Kernel/API.lean via the unified syscallEntry / syscallEntryChecked dispatch path. All 25 syscall-facing operations are routed through capability-gated dispatch with information-flow enforcement. The production entry point syscallEntryChecked enforces flow policies; the unchecked syscallEntry is retained for backward compatibility with existing proofs. All 25 syscalls have corresponding safe Rust wrappers in sele4n-sys.

structure.title: Project Structure
structure.lead: {{modules}} Lean modules, {{scripts}} test and CI scripts, and {{docs}} documentation files with specifications, ADRs, and roadmaps.

getting_started.title: Getting Started
getting_started.lead: Whether you want to explore the kernel model, contribute proofs, or understand the architecture — here is how to get up and running.
getting_started.step2_text: The setup script installs elan (the Lean version manager) with SHA-256 checksum verification and the pinned Lean 4.28.0 toolchain.
getting_started.step3_text: Build the entire kernel. All 2568 theorems are checked during compilation across 266 parallel build jobs. Zero sorry, zero axiom.
getting_started.step4_text: Start with the smoke test (minimum for PRs), then run the full suite for invariant work.
getting_started.step5_text: The rust/ workspace provides safe, no_std wrappers around all 25 seLe4n syscalls including notification signal/wait and reply-receive. Builds on both ARM64 (real trap) and x86_64 (mock trap for host-based testing).
getting_started.contributing_intro: seLe4n welcomes contributors. Before submitting a PR:
getting_started.contributing_item1: Read the Contributing Guide and Development Docs
getting_started.contributing_item2: Identify which area of the project your change addresses
getting_started.contributing_item3: Run ./scripts/test_smoke.sh at minimum; test_full.sh for theorem changes
getting_started.contributing_item4: Update invariants and theorems together with code changes
getting_started.contributing_item5: Keep PRs focused on one coherent unit of work
getting_started.contributing_item6: State transitions must have explicit success or failure outcomes
getting_started.documentation_intro: Comprehensive docs live in the docs/ directory:
getting_started.doc_item1: Master Specification — Complete kernel design
getting_started.doc_item2: Development Guide — Workflow and standards
getting_started.doc_item3: Testing Framework — Tier organization
getting_started.doc_item4: Threat Model — Security boundaries
getting_started.doc_item5: IF Roadmap — Non-interference proof status
getting_started.doc_item6: Claim & Evidence Index — Verification traceability
getting_started.doc_item7: Platform Binding ADR — Hardware abstraction design
getting_started.doc_item8: VSpace ADR — Memory model decisions

roadmap.title: Roadmap
roadmap.lead: Development is organized into milestones covering kernel semantics, audit remediation, performance, and hardware binding. Here is where the project stands.
roadmap.semantic_text: Scheduler semantics, capability/CSpace operations, IPC seeds, waiting handshake, lifecycle/retype, capability composition, service graphs, architecture boundary assumptions, and initial audit remediation.
roadmap.audit_text: Four complete audit portfolios: comprehensive audit, model/docs/maintainability, test validity and proof gaps, and test/CI and kernel hardening. All findings closed.
roadmap.remediation_text: Intrusive dual-queue IPC with message transfer verification. Untyped memory retype with watermark tracking. Information-flow completeness — 7-field ObservableState projection. Proof gap closure across all operations.
roadmap.perf_text: Migrated all hot paths to O(1) hash-based structures. 14 performance audit findings eliminated. Typed identifier Hashable instances, object store HashMap, ASID resolution table, RunQueue restructure, CNode slot HashMap, VSpace mapping HashMap, IPC dual-queue + notification, graph traversal optimization, and information-flow projection optimization. 400+ theorems re-verified.
roadmap.freeze_text: Implemented the Build → Freeze → Execute pipeline with 18 commutativity theorems proving frozen-phase semantic equivalence. FrozenOps subsystem with typed lookups, value-only mutations, and queue operations — all with isolation and preservation proofs.
roadmap.syscall_expansion_text: Expanded from 14 to 25 syscalls: added NotificationSignal (ID 14), NotificationWait (ID 15), and ReplyRecv (ID 16) compound IPC. Unified syscallEntry / syscallEntryChecked dispatch path with capability-gated routing. Rust ABI hardening with 25-variant SyscallId enum.
roadmap.rust_desc: Three no_std Rust crates (sele4n-types, sele4n-abi, sele4n-sys) providing safe wrappers around all 25 syscalls including notification signal/wait and reply-receive. Phantom-typed capabilities, ARM64 register ABI layer, W^X enforcement, IPC buffer overflow handling. Single unsafe block isolated to the svc #0 trap instruction.
roadmap.refinement_text:
Model fidelity — badge bitmask, per-thread registers, multi-level CSpace.
Invariant quality — tautology reclassification, adapter proof hooks.
Testing expansion — oracle, probe, and fixture coverage.
Cleanup — dead code removal, legacy/dual-queue resolution.
roadmap.rpi5_text: Structural foundation is in place: PlatformBinding typeclass, MachineConfig, VSpaceBackend, RPi5 stubs with BCM2712 memory map and GIC-400 constants. 48-bit VA / 44-bit PA address space. Blocked on refinement phase closure.
roadmap.convergence_text: Proof-to-platform convergence. Map model observability assumptions to RPi5 adapter obligations. Document unresolved security debt for side-channel follow-up.

footer.brand_text: A microkernel written in Lean 4 with machine-checked proofs.
